pattern: Binary search on the answer (earliest second), then greedily verify feasibility: work backwards through changeIndices to schedule decrement operations and markings.
hints: Binary search on time T in [1, m]; check if it's possible to mark all indices within T seconds.
For feasibility at time T: iterate seconds from T down to 1 — greedily use the last occurrence of each index in changeIndices as the marking second, and spend the remaining seconds decrementing.
Each index i needs nums[i] decrement operations plus 1 marking second; the total must fit within T — track a 'budget' of free decrement seconds as you scan backwards.

edge_inputs.description: all nums already zero — only need marking seconds
index never appears in changeIndices — impossible
barely enough time — all operations must be perfectly scheduled
changeIndices lists an index multiple times — use the latest occurrence for marking
not enough seconds to decrement before marking

stress.description: small random nums and changeIndices to stress the binary search + feasibility logic
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, min(size, 20))
    m = size
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 5) for _ in range(n)]
    # ensure every index appears at least once
    changeIndices = [rng.randint(1, n) for _ in range(m)]
    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        changeIndices[rng.randint(0, m - 1)] = i
    return [nums, changeIndices]
